title: Curtis Telvanni
shortDescription: Across worlds that were never meant to intersect, one figure appears again and again—unchanged in essence, altered only by circumstance. He is not reborn, nor replaced; he persists, carrying an awareness others cannot perceive. Each world believes itself whole, final, self-contained. Yet Curtis Telvanni moves through them quietly, studying patterns that repeat with unsettling precision. Names, empires, and technologies shift—but the underlying logic remains, and he follows it.

The name Telvanni is not his own. He took it from a distant place—an old, insular house of reclusive scholars who valued knowledge above all, where power was measured by intellect and will rather than allegiance. The philosophy stayed with him. It fit too well to discard. Since then, the name has followed him across worlds, not as identity, but as a chosen constant—something stable in a reality that refuses to be.

Somewhere beneath all worlds, something resembles a structure—vast, ordered, and unseen. Whether he moves within it freely or along paths already laid out, even he has not concluded. But he recognizes the motion, the logic, the inevitability of each shift. So he thinks. Always thinking. Because whatever binds him across worlds is not chance, nor fate—it is purpose, and it has yet to reveal itself.
